title: Terminal int8 hard-sigmoid retry-submission proof block
hypothesis: A fresh standalone int8 hard-sigmoid block under a unique canonical path can validate resolver-driven submission retry after a recoverable evaluator-side PR creation failure.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can one fresh bounded hard-sigmoid block prove that a recoverable submission failure can be diagnosed and resumed by the resolver without rerunning evaluation?
include: one standalone int8 hard-sigmoid block under a unique design path
one intentional recoverable submission interruption followed by resolver-driven retry
exclude: broad parameter sweeps beyond the single retry-submission candidate
follow_on_broad_sweep: none unless the resolver retry path still loses completion state or reviewable evidence

expected_benefit: proves that a finished proposal-backed Layer 1 run can be resumed through resolver-driven submission retry
adds one more bounded hard-sigmoid circuit_block reference under a unique canonical path
risks: this is primarily a workflow-proof item rather than a product-improvement item
the physical metrics may be redundant relative to the accepted hard-sigmoid references

expected_result.reason: the first goal is to complete one fresh bounded run and prove that resolver-driven submission retry can recover a recoverable PR creation failure without rerunning evaluation
